body.title: One store, one envelope — distinctions become tags, never new types, schemas, or stores
body.statement: All dev-process memory lives under devlab/runtime/memory/, written through memory_emit (the single chokepoint). A new distinction becomes a tag, a body field, or a category subdir — NEVER a new artifact type, a new MemoryType enum value, a new table, or a new store. The envelope shape (emitter/namespace/category/kind/links/body) is stable; new contracts (produced_by, validity_conditions) land as envelope fields on the existing shape. Retired paths (lab/, devlab/design_docs/, devlab/design_docs_for_igor/) are invalid write targets — the path-moves monitor alarms on them.
body.why: A renamed store with a surviving write-path silently splits the source of truth — decisions were written to dead lab/design_docs/ for days before anyone noticed. Tags are additive; schemas are fissive. One enforced home makes a tool writing elsewhere a detectable error instead of invisible drift.
